# iproute_link (Resource)

Manages a network link/interface (ip link). Supports 22 link types.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Dummy interface
resource "iproute_link" "dummy" {
  name = "dummy0"
  type = "dummy"
  dummy {}
}

# Bridge with STP and VLAN filtering
resource "iproute_link" "bridge" {
  name = "br0"
  type = "bridge"
  bridge {
    stp            = true
    vlan_filtering = true
    hello_time     = 200
  }
}

# Veth pair
resource "iproute_link" "veth" {
  name    = "veth0"
  type    = "veth"
  enabled = true
  mtu     = 9000
  veth {
    peer_name = "veth1"
  }
}

# VLAN interface
resource "iproute_link" "vlan" {
  name = "vlan100"
  type = "vlan"
  vlan {
    parent  = iproute_link.dummy.name
    vlan_id = 100
  }
}

# Bond with LACP
resource "iproute_link" "bond" {
  name = "bond0"
  type = "bond"
  bond {
    mode      = "802.3ad"
    miimon    = 100
    lacp_rate = "fast"
  }
}

# VxLAN overlay
resource "iproute_link" "vxlan" {
  name = "vxlan10"
  type = "vxlan"
  vxlan {
    vni   = 10
    group = "239.1.1.1"
    port  = 4789
    dev   = "eth0"
  }
}

# Macvlan
resource "iproute_link" "macvlan" {
  name = "macvlan0"
  type = "macvlan"
  macvlan {
    parent = iproute_link.dummy.name
    mode   = "bridge"
  }
}

# IPvlan
resource "iproute_link" "ipvlan" {
  name = "ipvlan0"
  type = "ipvlan"
  ipvlan {
    parent = iproute_link.dummy.name
    mode   = "l3"
  }
}

# Geneve tunnel
resource "iproute_link" "geneve" {
  name = "geneve0"
  type = "geneve"
  geneve {
    vni    = 100
    remote = "10.0.0.2"
    port   = 6081
  }
}

# GRE tunnel
resource "iproute_link" "gre" {
  name = "gre1"
  type = "gre"
  gre {
    local  = "10.0.0.1"
    remote = "10.0.0.2"
    ttl    = 64
  }
}

# SIT tunnel (IPv6-in-IPv4)
resource "iproute_link" "sit" {
  name = "sit1"
  type = "sit"
  sit {
    local  = "10.0.0.1"
    remote = "10.0.0.2"
  }
}

# IPIP tunnel
resource "iproute_link" "ipip" {
  name = "ipip1"
  type = "ipip"
  ipip {
    local  = "10.0.0.1"
    remote = "10.0.0.2"
    ttl    = 64
  }
}

# VTI tunnel
resource "iproute_link" "vti" {
  name = "vti1"
  type = "vti"
  vti {
    local  = "10.0.0.1"
    remote = "10.0.0.2"
    ikey   = 100
    okey   = 100
  }
}

# IPv6 tunnel
resource "iproute_link" "ip6tnl" {
  name = "ip6tnl1"
  type = "ip6tnl"
  ip6tnl {
    local  = "fd00::1"
    remote = "fd00::2"
  }
}

# WireGuard
resource "iproute_link" "wireguard" {
  name = "wg0"
  type = "wireguard"
  mtu  = 1420
  wireguard {
    private_key = "yAnz5TF+lXXJte14tji3zlMNq+hd2rYUIgJBgB3fBmk="
    listen_port = 51820
    peers {
      public_key  = "xTIBA5rboUvnH4htodjb6e697QjLERt1NAB4mZqp8Dg="
      endpoint    = "192.168.1.1:51820"
      allowed_ips = ["10.0.0.0/24", "10.0.1.0/24"]
      persistent_keepalive = 25
    }
  }
}

# TUN/TAP via link resource
resource "iproute_link" "tuntap" {
  name = "tap0"
  type = "tuntap"
  tuntap {
    mode        = "tap"
    multi_queue = true
  }
}

# IFB interface
resource "iproute_link" "ifb" {
  name = "ifb0"
  type = "ifb"
  ifb {}
}

# Bridge with port attached
resource "iproute_link" "br_port" {
  name    = "dummy1"
  type    = "dummy"
  master  = iproute_link.bridge.name
  enabled = true
  dummy {}
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Interface name (max 15 characters).
- `type` (String) Link type: dummy, bridge, veth, vlan, vxlan, bond, macvlan, macvtap, ipvlan, ifb, gre, gretap, ip6gre, ip6gretap, sit, vti, vti6, ip6tnl, ipip, geneve, wireguard, tuntap.

### Optional

- `bond` (Block, Optional) Bond-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--bond))
- `bridge` (Block, Optional) Bridge-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--bridge))
- `description` (String) Interface description (maps to ifAlias, RFC 8343).
- `dummy` (Block, Optional) Dummy interface (no configuration needed). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--dummy))
- `enabled` (Boolean) Administrative state (up/down). Defaults to true.
- `geneve` (Block, Optional) Geneve tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--geneve))
- `gre` (Block, Optional) GRE tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--gre))
- `ifb` (Block, Optional) IFB (Intermediate Functional Block) interface (no configuration needed). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--ifb))
- `ip6tnl` (Block, Optional) IP6TNL tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--ip6tnl))
- `ipip` (Block, Optional) IPIP tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--ipip))
- `ipvlan` (Block, Optional) IPVlan-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--ipvlan))
- `mac_address` (String) Hardware MAC address.
- `macvlan` (Block, Optional) Macvlan-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--macvlan))
- `macvtap` (Block, Optional) Macvtap-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--macvtap))
- `master` (String) Master device name (e.g., bridge name).
- `mtu` (Number) Maximum transmission unit.
- `sit` (Block, Optional) SIT tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--sit))
- `tuntap` (Block, Optional) TUN/TAP configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tuntap))
- `tx_queue_len` (Number) Transmit queue length.
- `veth` (Block, Optional) Veth-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--veth))
- `vlan` (Block, Optional) VLAN-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--vlan))
- `vti` (Block, Optional) VTI tunnel configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--vti))
- `vxlan` (Block, Optional) VxLAN-specific configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--vxlan))
- `wireguard` (Block, Optional) WireGuard configuration. Key/peer management via wgctrl. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--wireguard))

### Read-Only

- `admin_status` (String) Administrative status (up/down). RFC 8343.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `if_index` (Number) Interface index.
- `oper_status` (String) Operational status. RFC 8343.
- `speed` (Number) Interface speed in Mbps.
- `statistics` (Attributes) Interface statistics (computed). RFC 8343. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--statistics))

<a id="nestedblock--bond"></a>
### Nested Schema for `bond`

Optional:

- `down_delay` (Number) Delay before disabling slave (ms).
- `lacp_rate` (String) LACP rate (slow or fast).
- `miimon` (Number) MII monitoring interval (ms).
- `mode` (String) Bonding mode (balance-rr, active-backup, balance-xor, broadcast, 802.3ad, balance-tlb, balance-alb).
- `primary` (String) Primary slave interface.
- `up_delay` (Number) Delay before enabling slave (ms).
- `xmit_hash_policy` (String) Transmit hash policy.


<a id="nestedblock--bridge"></a>
### Nested Schema for `bridge`

Optional:

- `ageing_time` (Number) MAC ageing time (centiseconds).
- `default_pvid` (Number) Default PVID.
- `forward_delay` (Number) STP forward delay (centiseconds).
- `hello_time` (Number) STP hello time (centiseconds).
- `max_age` (Number) STP max age (centiseconds).
- `stp` (Boolean) Enable STP.
- `vlan_filtering` (Boolean) Enable VLAN filtering.

- `pmtu_disc` (Boolean) Enable PMTU discovery.

- `tos` (Number) TOS.
- `ttl` (Number) TTL.

Optional:

- `tos` (Number) TOS.
- `ttl` (Number) TTL.


<a id="nestedblock--ipvlan"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ipvlan`

Optional:

- `flag` (String) Flag: bridge, private, vepa.
- `mode` (String) Mode: l2, l3, l3s.
- `parent` (String) Parent interface name.


<a id="nestedblock--macvlan"></a>
### Nested Schema for `macvlan`

Optional:

- `mode` (String) Mode: private, vepa, bridge, passthru, source.
- `parent` (String) Parent interface name.


<a id="nestedblock--macvtap"></a>
### Nested Schema for `macvtap`

Optional:

- `mode` (String) Mode: private, vepa, bridge, passthru, source.
- `parent` (String) Parent interface name.


<a id="nestedblock--sit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sit`

Optional:

- `local` (String) Local endpoint IP.
- `remote` (String) Remote endpoint IP.
- `tos` (Number) TOS.
- `ttl` (Number) TTL.


<a id="nestedblock--tuntap"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tuntap`

Optional:

- `group` (Number) Group GID.
- `mode` (String) Mode: tun or tap.
- `multi_queue` (Boolean) Enable multi-queue.
- `owner` (Number) Owner UID.


<a id="nestedblock--veth"></a>
### Nested Schema for `veth`

Optional:

- `peer_name` (String) Peer interface name.


<a id="nestedblock--vlan"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vlan`

Optional:

- `parent` (String) Parent interface name.
- `protocol` (String) VLAN protocol (802.1Q or 802.1ad).
- `vlan_id` (Number) VLAN ID (1-4094).


<a id="nestedblock--vti"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vti`

Optional:

- `ikey` (Number) Input key.
- `local` (String) Local endpoint IP.
- `okey` (Number) Output key.
- `remote` (String) Remote endpoint IP.


<a id="nestedblock--vxlan"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vxlan`

Optional:

- `dev` (String) Physical device for tunnel endpoints.
- `group` (String) Multicast group address.
- `l2miss` (Boolean) Enable netlink LLADDR miss notifications.
- `l3miss` (Boolean) Enable netlink IP addr miss notifications.
- `learning` (Boolean) Enable MAC learning.
- `local` (String) Local IP address.
- `port` (Number) UDP destination port.
- `proxy` (Boolean) Enable ARP proxy.
- `vni` (Number) VxLAN Network Identifier.


<a id="nestedblock--wireguard"></a>
### Nested Schema for `wireguard`

Optional:

- `fwmark` (Number) Firewall mark.
- `listen_port` (Number) Listen port.
- `peers` (Attributes List) WireGuard peers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--wireguard--peers))
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) Private key.

<a id="nestedatt--wireguard--peers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `wireguard.peers`

Required:

- `public_key` (String) Public key.

Optional:

- `allowed_ips` (List of String) Allowed IP ranges.
- `endpoint` (String) Endpoint (host:port).
- `persistent_keepalive` (Number) Keepalive interval (seconds).
- `preshared_key` (String, Sensitive) Preshared key.

### Nested Schema for `statistics`

Read-Only:

- `rx_bytes` (Number)

- `rx_errors` (Number)
- `rx_packets` (Number)
- `tx_bytes` (Number)
- `tx_dropped` (Number)
- `tx_errors` (Number)
- `tx_packets` (Number)
